text:
It is unlikely to become physically addicted to heroin after a single use. Physical addiction typically develops after repeated use over time, as your body and brain adapt to the presence of the drug.

However, heroin is highly addictive, and even one use can create a powerful psychological craving or desire to use it again. Some people report an intense urge to repeat the experience after just one try. The risk of rapid addiction also depends on individual factors such as genetics, mental health, and environment.

In short: While physical addiction after one use is rare, trying heroin even once can set you on a dangerous path toward addiction.
